title: Generating 3D Scenes from Human Motion via Frontier Image Models
teaser: Given only a 3D human motion sequence, generate the 3D Gaussian Splat scene around it — using the SMPL body itself as a visual anchor for a frontier image model and lifting the result to 3D.
abstract:
Human motion implicitly encodes the 3D scene it takes place in: where someone sits implies a chair, how they reach implies a graspable surface, how they walk implies free floor space. This project builds a pipeline that takes only a 3D human motion sequence (SMPL parameters) and generates a 3D Gaussian Splat scene that the motion plausibly took place in.

The pipeline has two stages. First, the motion is described in two ways: a frozen motion captioner (e.g., MotionGPT-3) generates an action-level description ("a person sits down"), and a vision-language model (e.g., Qwen2-VL) applied to the rendered skeleton infers likely objects in the scene ("a chair, possibly a small table"). Second, a representative frame of the SMPL body is rendered and fed to a frontier image model (e.g., Flux Kontext), which generates the surrounding scene around it. The single 2D image is then made multi-view consistent (e.g., with Zero123++) and lifted to a 3D Gaussian Splat representation (e.g., with LGM). Finally, the full motion is animated inside the reconstructed scene to produce an orbiting-camera demo video.

The key idea is using the rendered SMPL body itself as a visual anchor for scene generation — a seated body forces a chair under the pelvis without needing explicit collision or contact losses. The method is evaluated against motion-to-scene baselines (SUMMON, MIME, Pose2Room, SHADE, DiffuScene) on the TRUMANS benchmark.
milestones: Set up the environment with a frontier image model (e.g., Flux Kontext) on the 3090, and reproduce one motion-to-scene baseline (e.g., Pose2Room or DiffuScene).
Build a motion preprocessing module that extracts contact points and free-space regions from the SMPL motion — these encode where furniture can and cannot go.

Generate two text descriptions per motion: an action-level caption from a frozen motion captioner (e.g., MotionGPT-3), and an object-level scene description from a frozen vision-language model (e.g., Qwen2-VL) applied to the rendered skeleton.
Render the SMPL body at a representative frame and use the image model to generate a single 2D scene around it, conditioned on the two text descriptions.
Lift the single image into a 3D Gaussian Splat scene by generating consistent multi-view images (e.g., with Zero123++) and reconstructing 3D from them (e.g., with LGM).
Evaluate scene quality against motion-to-scene baselines on TRUMANS and run a user study comparing orbiting-camera demo videos.

prerequisites.label: Advanced Deep Learning for Computer Vision: Visual Computing (ADL4CV)

references.label: Physics-based Scene Layout Generation from Human Motion (SIGGRAPH 2024)
DiffuScene — Denoising Diffusion Models for Generative Indoor Scene Synthesis (CVPR 2024)
TRUMANS dataset — Scaling Up Dynamic Human-Scene Interaction Modeling (CVPR 2024)
